title: Undercut-versus-overcut regime gating by race characteristics
summary: Official F1 strategy explainers note that undercut dominates on high-degradation tracks, while overcut becomes viable when tyre warm-up is poor and degradation is low, such as Monaco-like conditions.
rationale: Official F1 strategy explainers note that undercut dominates on high-degradation tracks, while overcut becomes viable when tyre warm-up is poor and degradation is low, such as Monaco-like conditions. That implies one global timing function is probably averaging across incompatible regimes.
implementation_hint: Infer a per-race regime from observable data only: early-stint LapTime_Delta slope, field-wide degradation spread, compound-specific warm-up behavior after recent stops, and race-level variance of fresh-tyre out-laps. Train separate specialists for undercut-friendly and overcut-friendly races, or add a regime probability that interacts with pit urgency features.
expected_effect: Should sharpen stop timing around tracks where the same tyre age means opposite actions depending on warm-up versus degradation balance.
risk: With only 26 races, hard race-level clustering can overfit. Use coarse 2-3 regimes and validate by grouped race-year folds.
